wow_comparison.delta: open_issues_total -15.5% (207→175); open_status -97.9% (48→1); assigned_analyst +10.0% (150→165); oldest_open_age -95.0% (7.83→0.39d); net_velocity +99 items/week (+6→+105)
wow_comparison.interpretation: PROP-031 BAU 3b is succeeding spectacularly on its primary metric (status='open' drained from 48 to 1, oldest open age from 7.83d to 0.39d, weekly closure rate more than doubled 60→133). The known side effect is load shift into assigned-analyst (now at 165 — operator_escalation tier per PROP-030 thresholds). This is the bottleneck PROP-031 designed-for: getting items OUT of decider's queue and INTO analyst's queue is the right direction, and analyst Mode 1 dispatcher (PROP-029) is engineered for high throughput. PROP-032 will further tighten routing correctness in this same direction.

findings.description: PROP-030 operator_escalation tier fires: assigned-analyst count is 165 (>150 threshold). This is a structural artifact of PROP-031 BAU 3b's success: items previously stuck in status='open' for 7+ days are now being routed promptly to analyst. Net-velocity is strongly positive (+105/week) — the system IS draining work, just relocating the bottleneck from decider to analyst. NOT a stalled-pipeline situation; closing more than opening.
findings.evidence: queue-history.jsonl: 2026-05-12T05:14 assigned_analyst=150; 2026-05-13T02:41 assigned_analyst=165 (+15 in 21h). Open status dropped 48→1 over same window — items are flowing through. closed_issues_velocity_7d doubled (60→133). The 7 items the operator manually rerouted from pending-human → assigned-analyst on 2026-05-12 contribute ~5% of the 165 figure; the remainder is BAU 3b doing its routing job correctly.
findings.suggested_fix: No-action this run. The metric's threshold flagging is correct (>150 IS an escalation candidate) but the underlying state is by-design transitional. Watch next 2-3 tinker runs: if assigned-analyst keeps climbing past 200 with no analyst-side drain, escalate via HNOTE recommending analyst cadence bump or analyst Mode 1 batch-size tuning. If assigned-analyst plateaus and starts dropping (analyst eats faster than decider routes), no action needed.

directive_outcome.title: BAU 3b matrix step 1 over-escalation tightening
tasks_completed: task_1_define_operator_only_judgment_criteria — five OOJ categories enumerated (verdict commit on operator-authored material, NEVER_PUSH file, legal/strategic/external-comms, physical-world/operator-side knowledge, pipeline-config). Plus four explicitly-NOT-operator-only categories (citation precision, strawman defense, narrow precision, verdict-change PROPOSAL drafting) for calibration.

task_2_redesign_matrix_step_1 — three-context branch table specified (M1 stale-sweep / M3 carry-over / BAU 3b) with PRESERVED severity invariant for M1+M3 and replaced OOJ-criteria check for BAU 3b. Single canonical insertion point in routing-matrix.md identified.

task_3_specify_LLM_check — five enumerated OOJ categories with one-line trigger phrases + worked examples (in-scope and out-of-scope per category). Includes the four directive-provided examples (ISS-1944, ISS-1921, test.js NEVER_PUSH, decider-mode.json flip).

task_4_retroactive_simulation — All 7 items (ISS-1921, 1933, 1944, 1945, 1948, 1957, 1958) walked through proposed BAU step 1 + matrix steps 2-6. Result: 7/7 route to analyst with operator-matched class_hints. Zero escalate-to-human. Verdict: PASS.

task_5_carve_out_what_NOT_to_do — explicit preserve list (M1 step 1, M3 step 1, steps 2-6, narrowness gate, class-hint chain, ledger schema, override path) and explicit do-NOT list (no compound severity-AND-category logic, no file-structure rewrite, no decider.md changes beyond one-line pointer).
